records.snippet_en: ajority of run-off will occur before 2030. 4 GHGs other than CO2 are excluded. Emission factors are sourced from the IPCC Guidelines for National GHG Inventories (2006). 2023 2022 2021 2020 2019 Target Canada Loans outstanding ($ millions) $1,926 $1,390 $2,425 $3,003 $2,917 Scope 1 and 2 Scope 1 and 2 financed emissions (ktCO2e) 357 347 665 1,146 887 Portfolio PCAF data quality score 2.9 2.0 1.7 1.7 2.1 Economic emissions intensity (tCO2e/$ millions of loans outstanding) 186 250 274 382 304 Physical emissions intensity (tCO2e/TJ) 5.1 6.4 5.6 5.9 5.3 33% reduction by 2030 Rest of world3 Loans outstanding ($ millions) $141 $656 $2,046 $5,120 $5,636 Scope 1 and 2 Scope 1 and 2 financed emissions (ktCO2e) 28 159 605 1,210 1,382 Portfolio PCAF data quality score 3.1 3.2 3.6 3.5 4.0 Economic emissions intensity (tCO2e/$ millions of loans outstanding) 203 242 296 236 245 Physical emissions intensity (tCO2e/TJ) 2.7 3.0 3.2 3.0 3.4 33% reduction by 2030 Global4 Loans outstanding ($ millions) $2,067 $2,046 $4,471 $8,123 $8,553 Downstream Scope 3 Financed emissions (ktCO2) 5,393 6,852 19,320 37,645 38,914 24% reduction by 2030 has been achieved Portfolio PCAF data quality score 3.0 3.1 3.3 3.1 3.4 Economic emissions intensity (tCO2/$ millions of loans outstanding) 2,610 3,350 4,321 4,635 4,550 Physical emissions intensity (tCO2/TJ) 66.5 64.4 62.6 63.2 62.2 Upstream oil and gas – Global1,2 that result from the combustion of products produced by the same oil and gas borrowers. Our target to reduce absolute Scope 3 financed emissions by 24% by 2030 has been achieved. Details of our financed emissions methodology, including measurement approach, inputs and assumptions, can be found in our GHG Reporting Methodology – Financed emissions.
